pending_context.whisper: Coleman / Cleaver and BPP East-West Coast schism
pending_context.notes: Pass-2 catalogs Howard's first-person framing of the 1971 BPP East-West split; cross-reference with Aaron Dixon #1 / Elmer Dixon #37

pending_context.whisper: "City College in Oakland" / "enter into City College in Oakland and draw my GI bill"
pending_context.candidate_correction: Oakland City College (pre-Merritt name) — likely the same institution as #35.3
pending_context.notes: "I decided to enter into City College in Oakland and draw my GI bill." Howard later says he met Newton and Seale at "Mary College" (Merritt). Historical context: Oakland City College was the parent institution that in 1964 split into Merritt College and Laney College — the Grove Street campus where Newton/Seale/Howard met became Merritt. Howard's "City College in Oakland" reference (used for GI Bill matriculation) and his later "Mary College" reference may both point to the same Grove Street campus, captured at slightly different historical names. Pass 4 catalogs this as a Whisper-stable institutional-name ambiguity rather than a transcription error per se.

pending_context.whisper: "New Orleans, California" / "air base in New Orleans, California"
pending_context.candidate_correction: (likely) Travis Air Force Base or Hamilton Air Force Base, California
pending_context.notes: "I was still in the service on the air base in New Orleans, California." There is no Air Force base in New Orleans, California (no such locality exists). Howard's late-1950s Air Force discharge in the Bay Area is most likely a reference to Hamilton Air Force Base (Marin County, active 1932–88) or Travis AFB (Fairfield, active throughout the period); "New Orleans" is a Whisper hallucination on a hard-to-parse base name. Adversarial verification needed for which specific base.

pending_context.whisper: "Sky's Olympic" / "stuff like that for Sky's Olympic"
pending_context.notes: "people of New Haven were frightened … we said stuff like that for Sky's Olympic, and they better not happen to Bobby or New Haven being ashes." Speaker is recounting BPP rhetoric to New Haven officials during the 1970-71 Seale-Huggins trial; the threat phrase is along the lines of "the skies are going to fall" / "the skies will split" / "the heavens will break." Possibly also "scorched-earth" framing. Speaker-originating rhetorical construction.

pending_context.whisper: "GIF from Huey" / "Brown became the chairman of GIF from Huey"
pending_context.notes: "Brown became the chairman of GIF from Huey. But, other than that, it was nothing." Context is Elaine Brown's 1974 assumption of BPP chairship during Huey Newton's Cuban exile (already-corrected Pass 1 #35.45). The "GIF" token is most likely a Whisper rendering of "the gig" (i.e., "Brown became the chairman in the gig handed off from Huey") or "the chair from Huey." Speaker-originating shorthand.

"we had some panthers in those classes, and one day it kind of exploded, and they jumped up and knocked him under the ass in the classroom." Speech-rendering Whisper near-miss for the colloquial phrase "knocked him on his ass." Minor; speaker-originating dialect.

pending_context.whisper: "in Skoglade" / "dynamite crew to help him in Skoglade"
pending_context.notes: "Sam … had put together a dynamite crew to help him in Skoglade. And he said, stuff like, circle like to educate, get the papers out." The "Skoglade" token does not match any canonical NYC neighborhood; possibly a corrupted rendering of "in shaking it loose" or a specific subway/distribution-route reference. Adversarial verification needed.

pending_context.whisper: "Bill Hicks got a collection" (BPP newspaper collector)
pending_context.notes: "if you were found one [old issue of *The Black Panther*] somewhere, it would cost you over $100. But, here. Our friend Bill Hicks got a collection." Pass 1 caught Billy X Jennings (35.44) but "Bill Hicks" is a separate reference earlier in the transcript. Possibly the same person ("Billy X" / "Bill X" / "Bill Hicks") or a distinct BPP-archive collector. Adversarial verification needed for whether "Bill Hicks" is a canonical BPP-newspaper archivist or a Whisper rendering of "Billy X."

pending_context.whisper: "regiment was, you know, the mandate" / "they started to develop the 10 point platform and program, which was, you know, the regiment, you know, if you will, that would differentiate us"
pending_context.notes: "they started to develop the 10 point platform and program, which was, you know, the regiment, you know, if you will, that would differentiate us from other organizations." Speaker is referring to the BPP foundational manifesto; "regiment" is a Whisper rendering of "the regimen" or, more likely, "the document." Speaker-originating ambiguity.

pending_context.whisper: "I had a brilliant thing to do" / "what did you do to move on to focus on at that point"
pending_context.notes: "I had a brilliant thing to do. We had other people stepping in because Eldridge, he was right off from down in." Context establishes Howard is describing what he did AFTER Cleaver took over editing duties — "brilliant thing" doesn't fit the meaning, which is "I had some free time" or "I had a window." Speaker-originating elision.
